subject: Transfer Step 5 (apply split) into combined notebook
description: Add a new markdown section '## Step 5: Apply Split' after Step 4's cells, transferring the train/val/test split-assignment logic from pre_csd_5_apply_split.py's run_step() (02_thesis_data/_02_preprocessing/nielsen/CSD/pipeline_step_scripts/pre_csd_5_apply_split.py, lines 110-178) -- this is the time-based split, grain-independent, using TRAIN_END/VAL_END boundaries. Strip input_parquet_for_grain()/output_parquet_for_grain() (lines 99-109) and main()/argparse (lines 179+). Pass in-memory `df_features` from Task 5 directly in, producing `df_split` with a split-label column (train/val/test). Display the resulting split sizes (row counts, brand counts per split) directly next to the EDA's existing Cell 13 'Train/Val/Test Split Analysis' table -- this is another case where combining EDA-derived boundaries with their actual application closes a visibility gap the P0029 analysis flagged (Section 3: full-dataset vs split-aware parameter derivation). Flag clearly in a markdown cell whether TRAIN_END/VAL_END here are still hardcoded from the EDA's Cell 13 print statement or wired to read the EDA's own computed values from earlier in the same notebook run (prefer the latter now that everything is in one notebook -- ask Brian if unsure which he wants). Verify by comparing df_split's split sizes against a fresh `python pre_csd_5_apply_split.py --grain bymonth` run's step_5 output parquet.
activeForm: Transferring Step 5 (apply split) into combined notebook
